req.title: Announcement rollout — variant 3
req.statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-089 defines three testable concerns: (1) happy-path publishing with valid department selection, (2) rejection with no departments selected (422), and (3) a boundary condition of exactly one department selected (minimum valid selection). A fourth test covers the webhook emission obligation. EP partitions valid vs invalid department sets; BVA probes the exact minimum (1 department); NEG validates the 422 error pathway; STC traces the draft→published state transition including the side-effect webhook event.

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_with_multiple_departments_succeeds
preconditions: At least two departments exist in the org
No duplicate announcement with same title exists
actions: Fill in announcement title and body
Select two or more departments from the department picker
expected: Announcement appears in the org-announcements list
Selected department employees are marked as notified
Response body contains announcement id and status='published'
trace: R-WEB-089: variant 3 semantics — multi-department selection accepted
tests.executable:
def test_publish_announcement_with_multiple_departments_succeeds(admin_session, dept_factory, announcement_cleanup):
    dept_a = dept_factory.create(name='Engineering')
    dept_b = dept_factory.create(name='Marketing')
    payload = {
        'title': 'Q3 All-Hands Notice',
        'body': 'Please attend the all-hands.',
        'department_ids': [dept_a['id'], dept_b['id']]
    }
    resp = admin_session.post('/org-announcements', json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['status'] == 'published'
    assert 'id' in data
    announcement_cleanup.register(data['id'])
    detail = admin_session.get(f"/org-announcements/{data['id']}").json()
    notified_depts = {d['id'] for d in detail['departments']}
    assert dept_a['id'] in notified_depts
    assert dept_b['id'] in notified_depts

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_with_no_departments_returns_422
actions: Fill in announcement title and body
expected: Response body contains validation error referencing department_ids field
No announcement record is created in the database
trace: R-WEB-089: error pathway — 422 when no departments selected
tests.executable:
def test_publish_announcement_with_no_departments_returns_422(admin_session, assert_no_announcement_created):
    payload = {
        'title': 'Empty Dept Announcement',
        'body': 'This should fail.',
        'department_ids': []
    }
    resp = admin_session.post('/org-announcements', json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    error_body = resp.json()
    field_errors = [e.get('field', '') for e in error_body.get('errors', [])]
    assert any('department' in f for f in field_errors), (
        f'Expected department field error, got: {error_body}'
    )
    assert_no_announcement_created(title='Empty Dept Announcement')

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_with_exactly_one_department_succeeds
preconditions: Exactly one department is available or created for this test
actions: Fill in announcement title and body
Select exactly one department — the minimum valid boundary
expected: HTTP 201 received — one department is sufficient
Exactly one department listed in announcement detail
trace: R-WEB-089: BVA lower boundary — minimum valid department_ids count is 1
tests.executable:
def test_publish_announcement_with_exactly_one_department_succeeds(admin_session, dept_factory, announcement_cleanup):
    dept = dept_factory.create(name='SingleDept')
    payload = {
        'title': 'Single Dept Boundary Test',
        'body': 'One department only.',
        'department_ids': [dept['id']]
    }
    resp = admin_session.post('/org-announcements', json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['status'] == 'published'
    announcement_cleanup.register(data['id'])
    detail = admin_session.get(f"/org-announcements/{data['id']}").json()
    assert len(detail['departments']) == 1
    assert detail['departments'][0]['id'] == dept['id']

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_emits_webhook_event
preconditions: A webhook endpoint is registered for announcement.published events
Webhook capture stub is running and accessible
actions: Fill in announcement title and body
Poll webhook capture stub for the emitted event
expected: Announcement transitions from draft to published state (STC: draft→published)
Webhook capture stub receives exactly one announcement.published event
Webhook payload contains announcement id, org_id, and department_ids
trace: R-WEB-089: action must emit a webhook event for external consumers
R-WEB-089: STC — state transition draft → published triggers webhook side-effect
tests.executable:
def test_publish_announcement_emits_webhook_event(admin_session, dept_factory, webhook_stub, announcement_cleanup):
    dept = dept_factory.create(name='WebhookDept')
    payload = {
        'title': 'Webhook Emission Test',
        'body': 'Verifying webhook side-effect.',
        'department_ids': [dept['id']]
    }
    resp = admin_session.post('/org-announcements', json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    ann_id = resp.json()['id']
    announcement_cleanup.register(ann_id)
    event = webhook_stub.wait_for_event(
        event_type='announcement.published',
        timeout=10
    )
    assert event is not None, 'No webhook event received within timeout'
    assert event['event'] == 'announcement.published'
    assert str(event['data']['announcement_id']) == str(ann_id)
    assert dept['id'] in event['data']['department_ids']
